message:
Remove unused last_error variable and replace with structured tracing

Clean up TUI error handling by removing the unused last_error variable and
replacing its assignments with proper structured tracing for search errors.

What changed:
- Removed last_error: Option<String> variable declaration (line 224)
- Removed last_error = None assignment on successful search (line 830)
- Removed last_error = Some(format!("Search error: {err}")) on search failure
- Added tracing::warn!("search error: {err}") to log search errors with context

Problem identified:
- last_error variable was declared and assigned but never actually used
- No UI rendering code displayed last_error to the user
- Footer status message already shows "Search error (see footer)." on failure
- Variable was pure dead code accumulating state without any consumer

Why this is better:
- Structured tracing provides better debugging than unused in-memory state
- tracing::warn! logs include timestamp, module, and can be filtered/forwarded
- Reduces memory footprint (eliminates unnecessary String allocation)
- Simplifies state management (one less variable to track across event loop)
- Aligns with established logging pattern already used throughout TUI module

Technical details:
- Search error path now logs via tracing::warn! before clearing results
- Log message format: "search error: {err}" where {err} is the anyhow::Error
- Tracing spans from parent functions provide additional context (query, filters)
- Status footer still shows user-friendly "Search error (see footer)." message
- Error logging happens on search failure before results.clear() to preserve context

Error visibility improvements:
- With tracing: errors appear in logs with full context and can be aggregated
- With last_error: errors were stored but never displayed (dead code)
- Developers debugging search issues can now see errors in log output
- Users still see the same footer status message (no UX change)

Log output example:
```
WARN coding_agent_search::ui::tui: search error: Tantivy index corrupted
```

This change eliminates dead code while improving observability through proper
structured logging. No functional or UX changes—purely internal cleanup.
